facts.injury: a worsened condition requiring further surgery
facts.screening_panel_stage: the screening panel was unable to reach unanimity

docket.entry: Notice of claim served, initiating the mandatory prelitigation screening panel process.
Prelitigation screening panel convened; chair appointed and submissions ordered.
Complaint and demand for jury trial filed after the screening-panel process.
Answers, cross-claim, and third-party complaint against the consulting provider filed.
Scheduling order entered; discovery and expert deadlines set.
Order compelling production of the credentialing file.
Expert disclosures exchanged; discovery continues toward trial.

motions.title: Motion to bifurcate liability and damages
motions.relief: Separate trials on liability and damages.

motions.title: Motion regarding the admissibility of the screening-panel findings
motions.relief: An order on whether and how the prelitigation screening panel findings are admissible. 24 M.R.S. § 2853.

motions.title: Motion to compel the credentialing file
motions.relief: An order compelling production of the defendant physician's credentialing and privileging file.

motions.title: Motion for partial summary judgment on informed consent
motions.relief: Judgment that the informed-consent claim fails as a matter of law.

motions.title: Motion in limine to exclude a plaintiff expert
motions.relief: Exclusion of an unreliable expert opinion under Daubert and M.R.E. 702.

causes_of_action.claim: Professional negligence (departure from the standard of care)
causes_of_action.governing_law: Professional negligence; 24 M.R.S. § 2902; expert testimony required
elements: A physician-patient relationship and a duty to meet the standard of care
A breach of the applicable standard of care
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Compensatory damages for past and future medical care, lost earning capacity, and pain and suffering

elements: An agency or employment relationship with the providers
Negligence within the scope of that relationship
Independent negligence in credentialing, supervision, or retention
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Compensatory damages against the hospital and health system

elements: A duty to meet the standard of care
A breach of that standard by the consulting provider
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Compensatory damages against the consulting provider

causes_of_action.governing_law: Informed consent; the duty to disclose material risks and alternatives
elements: A duty to disclose material risks and alternatives
A reasonable patient would have declined the treatment
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Compensatory damages for the consequences of the undisclosed risk

affirmative_defenses.defense: Care met the standard of care
affirmative_defenses.basis: The treatment conformed to the accepted standard of care for the specialty.
The action was not commenced within the three-year limitations period. 24 M.R.S. § 2902.
The injury was a recognized risk or complication of the procedure, not the result of negligence.

The action was not commenced within the med-mal-specific limitations period of 24 M.R.S. ch. 21; the general 14 M.R.S. § 752 6-year period does not apply. NOTE: the exact period and accrual rule should be verified against current statute.
